1	/// Used to do reference-to-value conversions thus not consuming the input value.
2	///
3	/// This is mainly used with [`State`] to extract "substates" from a reference to main application
4	/// state. See ["Sharing state with handlers"][sharing-state] for a worked example.
5	///
6	/// See [`State`] for more details on how library authors should use this trait.
7	///
8	/// This trait can be derived using `#[derive(FromRef)]`.
9	///
10	/// [`State`]: https://docs.rs/axum/0.8/axum/extract/struct.State.html
11	/// [sharing-state]: https://docs.rs/axum/0.8/axum/index.html#sharing-state-with-handlers
12	// NOTE: This trait is defined in axum-core, even though it is mainly used with `State` which is
13	// defined in axum. That allows crate authors to use it when implementing extractors.
14	pub trait FromRef<T> {
15	    /// Converts to this type from a reference to the input type.
16	    fn from_ref(input: &T) -> Self;
17	}
18	
19	impl<T> FromRef<T> for T
20	where
21	    T: Clone,
22	{
23	    fn from_ref(input: &T) -> Self {
24	        input.clone()
25	    }
26	}

## Step 1: Handler trait implementations

**Trait definition:** `axum/src/handler/mod.rs:148` — `pub trait Handler<T, S>: Clone + Send + Sync + Sized + 'static` with `call(self, req: Request, state: S) -> Self::Future` (line 153) and `layer(...)` adapter (189) producing `Layered`. `with_state(self, state)` (202) yields `HandlerService`.

**Type-state markers:** `T` is a coherence-rule discriminator encoding the handler arg-tuple shape (`((),)`, `(M, T1,…, Tn, Tlast)`, `IntoResponseHandler`, etc.). `S` is the app state type. `M` is an inner marker (`ViaRequest`/`ViaParts`) for `FromRequest` selection.

All Handler impls (exhaustive):

1. `axum/src/handler/mod.rs:208` — `impl<F, Fut, Res, S> Handler<((),), S> for F where F: FnOnce() -> Fut`. Zero-arg closure. No extractors run; just `Box::pin(async move { self().await.into_response() })` at line 217.
2. `axum/src/handler/mod.rs:227` (macro `impl_handler`, expanded for arities 1..=16 by `all_the_tuples!` at line 262) — `impl<F, Fut, S, Res, M, T1..Tn, Tlast> Handler<(M, T1,…, Tlast,), S> for F`. Splits `req.into_parts()` (239); runs each `T::from_request_parts(&mut parts, &state)` sequentially returning `rejection.into_response()` on `Err` (242–245); rebuilds `Request::from_parts(parts, body)` (248); runs `Tlast::from_request(req, &state)` (250); then **user closure invoked at line 255**: `self($($ty,)* $last,).await.into_response()`.
3. `axum/src/handler/mod.rs:271` — `impl<T, S> Handler<private::IntoResponseHandler, S> for T where T: IntoResponse + Clone`. No extractors; returns `ready(self.into_response())` (278). This is how `get("Hello, World!")` works.
4. `axum/src/handler/mod.rs:317` — `impl<H, S, T, L> Handler<T, S> for Layered<L, H, T, S>`. Created by `Handler::layer`. Inside `call` (329): wraps the inner handler with state (`self.handler.with_state(state)` → `HandlerService`), applies `self.layer.layer(svc)`, then `svc.oneshot(req).map(...)`. The user's closure ultimately runs via the wrapped `HandlerService`'s `Service::call` which delegates to `Handler::call` on the inner handler.
5. `axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1355` — `impl<S> Handler<(), S> for MethodRouter<S>`. Lets a `MethodRouter` itself be a `Handler`. `call` (1361) delegates to `self.call_with_state(req, state)` wrapped in `InfallibleRouteFuture`.
6. `axum-extra/src/handler/mod.rs:167` — `impl<H, T, S> Handler<T, S> for IntoHandler<H, T, S> where H: HandlerCallWithExtractors<T, S>, T: FromRequest<S>`. Runs `T::from_request(req, &state).await` once (179), then `self.handler.call(t, state).await` (180); user closure invoked indirectly via the `HandlerCallWithExtractors` impl (axum-extra/src/handler/mod.rs:120, expanded for 0..=16 tuples) at line 134: `self($($ty,)*).map(IntoResponse::into_response)`.
7. `axum-extra/src/handler/or.rs:56` — `impl<S, L, R, Lt, Rt, M> Handler<(M, Lt, Rt), S> for Or<L, R, Lt, Rt, S>`. `call` (69): first tries `Lt::from_request_parts(&mut parts, &state).await` (73); on `Ok` runs `self.lhs.call(lt, state).await`; on `Err`, reassembles request and tries `Rt::from_request(req, &state).await` (79), then `self.rhs.call(rt, state).await`. User closure invoked via the `HandlerCallWithExtractors` impl of each branch.

**Bridge to Tower:** `HandlerService<H, T, S>` (`axum/src/handler/service.rs:22`) — `impl<H, T, S, B> Service<Request<B>> for HandlerService<H, T, S>` (146). Its `call` (165) clones the handler, invokes `Handler::call(handler, req, self.state.clone())`, and maps the future to `Ok` (171–172). `poll_ready` is always `Ready(Ok(()))` (158).

## Step 2: Extractor chain

**Trait definitions** (`axum-core/src/extract/mod.rs`):

- `FromRequestParts<S>` (line 53): `from_request_parts(parts: &mut Parts, state: &S) -> impl Future<Output=Result<Self, Self::Rejection>> + Send`. Cannot consume body; runs in any order, can run multiple times.
- `FromRequest<S, M = private::ViaRequest>` (line 79): `from_request(req: Request, state: &S) -> …`. Consumes body; runs once and must be the last arg.
- Coherence markers: `private::ViaParts` and `private::ViaRequest` (32–37).
- Blanket: `impl<S, T> FromRequest<S, ViaParts> for T where T: FromRequestParts<S>` (line 91) — auto-promotes any `FromRequestParts` to `FromRequest`, which is how the last-arg slot in `impl_handler!` (axum/src/handler/mod.rs:234, `$last: FromRequest<S, M> + Send`) accepts either kind via the `M` marker.
- Wrappers: `Result<T, T::Rejection>` impls (107, 119) make rejection-handling opt-in with `Infallible` rejection.
- `Option<T>` impls live in `axum-core/src/extract/option.rs:41`/`58`, driven by `OptionalFromRequestParts` / `OptionalFromRequest` traits.

**`FromRef` and `State`** (`axum-core/src/extract/from_ref.rs:14`): trait `FromRef<T> { fn from_ref(input: &T) -> Self }` with a blanket `impl<T: Clone> FromRef<T> for T` (line 19). `State<S>` is at `axum/src/extract/state.rs:301`; its `FromRequestParts<OuterState>` impl (303) takes `&OuterState`, calls `InnerState::from_ref(state)` (314), wraps in `State`. Rejection: `Infallible`.

**Rejection plumbing:** Each `From*` impl declares `type Rejection: IntoResponse`. In `Handler` impl 2 (`axum/src/handler/mod.rs:242–245, 250–253`), `Err(rejection)` is mapped to `return rejection.into_response()` — i.e., the rejection becomes the response and the user closure never runs. Composite rejections are built with the `composite_rejection!` and `define_rejection!` macros (`axum-core/src/extract/rejection.rs:8, 40`; `axum-core/src/macros.rs`).

**Built-in extractors (file:line):**

| Extractor | File:line | Trait | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|

| `Bytes`, `String`, `BytesMut`, `Body` | `axum-core/src/extract/request_parts.rs:100, 118, 69, 164` | `FromRequest<S>` | body-consuming |

| `WebSocketUpgrade` | `axum/src/extract/ws.rs:442` | `FromRequestParts<S>` | |

## Step 3: Serve-to-response lifecycle (7 layers)

1. **`axum::serve(listener, make_service)`** — `axum/src/serve/mod.rs:103`. Returns `Serve<L, M, S, B, TokioExecutor>`. `Serve::run` (line 321) loops calling `listener.accept().await` (line 333) then `handle_connection(...)` (334). Graceful variant: `WithGracefulShutdown::run` at line 447.

4. **`Router::call` → `Router::call_with_state`** — `axum/src/routing/mod.rs:599` defines `Service<Request<B>> for Router<()>`; `call` at 614 wraps body and delegates to `self.call_with_state(req, ())`. `call_with_state` (line 452) calls `self.inner.path_router.call_with_state(...)` and falls through to `catch_all_fallback.call_with_state` (458) on `NotFound`.
5. **`PathRouter::call_with_state`** — `axum/src/routing/path_router.rs:325`. Sets `OriginalUri` in extensions (334–337), calls `self.node.at(parts.uri.path())` (matchit, 342), inserts `MatchedPath` via `set_matched_path_for_request` (347) and url params via `insert_url_params` (353), looks up the endpoint, and either calls `method_router.call_with_state(req, state)` (363) or `route.clone().call_owned(req)` (365).
6. **`MethodRouter::call_with_state`** — `axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1167`. Matches `req.method()` (1174) and dispatches to one of the per-method `MethodEndpoint`s: `Route(route)` → `route.clone().oneshot_inner_owned($req)` (1178); `BoxedHandler(handler)` → `handler.clone().into_route(state)` then `oneshot_inner_owned` (1181–1182). The future bubbles back through `RouteFuture` (`axum/src/routing/future.rs`) to hyper, which serializes the `Response`.

## Step 4: Adding a request context / ID layer

**Recommendation: Extension-layer approach** (least breaking).

Why: axum already has the precedent — `MatchedPath` and `OriginalUri` are written into `parts.extensions` *inside* `PathRouter::call_with_state` (`axum/src/routing/path_router.rs:334–337` for `OriginalUri`; `347` for `MatchedPath` via `set_matched_path_for_request`). Extractors then read from `parts.extensions`:

- `MatchedPath` extractor: `axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:73–78` reads `parts.extensions.get::<Self>()`.
- `Extension<T>` extractor: `axum/src/extension.rs:83–98` reads `parts.extensions.get::<T>()`.

So the existing pattern for "request-scoped data threaded everywhere" *is* request extensions. A request-ID layer should follow the same playbook.

**Edit map (least-breaking — new module, no touch to Handler/Router internals):**

1. **New extractor + ID type** — new file `axum/src/extract/request_id.rs`:
   - `pub struct RequestId(Arc<str>)` with `as_str()` accessor (mirroring `MatchedPath` at `axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:57`).
   - `impl<S: Send + Sync> FromRequestParts<S> for RequestId` reading `parts.extensions.get::<Self>()` (mirror `axum/src/extension.rs:83`); rejection variant `RequestIdMissing` defined via `define_rejection!` (`axum-core/src/macros.rs`).
   - `impl<S> OptionalFromRequestParts<S> for RequestId` returning `Ok(extensions.get::<Self>().cloned())` (mirror matched_path.rs:84).
2. **Wire the extractor module** — `axum/src/extract/mod.rs:14` (add `mod request_id;`), `axum/src/extract/mod.rs:30` (re-export `pub use self::request_id::RequestId;`).
3. **Rejection types** — `axum/src/extract/rejection.rs` gains a `RequestIdMissing` rejection following the file's existing `composite_rejection!` / `define_rejection!` patterns.
4. **`SetRequestIdLayer` middleware** — new file `axum/src/middleware/request_id.rs` implementing `tower_layer::Layer<S>` whose service reads/generates an ID (from a header like `x-request-id`, falling back to a uuid) and inserts it into `req.extensions_mut()` before delegating to the inner service. Pattern matches what `axum/src/middleware/from_fn.rs` and `axum/src/middleware/map_request.rs` already do.
5. **Optional: integrate into `Router`** by exposing `Router::with_request_id_layer()` as sugar, applied via existing `Router::layer` (`axum/src/routing/mod.rs:296`). Not strictly necessary — `router.layer(SetRequestIdLayer::new())` already works because `Router::layer`'s bound is `Layer<Route>`.

**Tradeoffs (axum-specific surfaces):**

- **`Router::layer` vs `Router::route_layer`** (`axum/src/routing/mod.rs:296` vs `313`): `layer` applies to *every* request including 404/405 fallbacks (the layer is applied to both `path_router` and `catch_all_fallback`, lines 305–307). `route_layer` (321) only wraps matched routes — it skips the `catch_all_fallback` branch. For a request-ID layer you want IDs on 404s too, so **use `Router::layer`** (or a top-level `serve`-level wrap), matching the `MatchedPath`/`OriginalUri` pattern of running *before* path matching.
- **Layer ordering vs path-router internals**: `MatchedPath` is set inside `PathRouter::call_with_state` (`axum/src/routing/path_router.rs:347`), so any user `Router::layer` runs *before* `MatchedPath` is in extensions. A request-ID layer added via `Router::layer` is fine because it only needs to *insert* an extension and doesn't need `MatchedPath`. If you want the request-ID to be available in span fields *along with* matched path (e.g. for tracing), you compose: `Router::layer(TraceLayer::new_for_http().make_span_with(|req| { ... }))` where the span function pulls both.
- **Handler trait modification (rejected):** would require changing the trait signature at `axum/src/handler/mod.rs:148` plus every impl listed in Step 1 (six core impls plus the macro expansion for 16 arities, plus `axum-extra` impls). Cascades into `HandlerService` at `axum/src/handler/service.rs:165`. Hard breaking change for zero user benefit since extensions already thread through.
- **New extractor type only, no layer (rejected):** would force every handler to also accept a `RequestId` extractor without anything populating it. Without the layer, `FromRequestParts` returns `Err(RequestIdMissing)` and you get a 5xx — same failure mode as forgetting `.layer(Extension(...))`.

The extension-layer approach reuses every existing surface (`Router::layer`, `parts.extensions`, the `FromRequestParts` blanket promotion to `FromRequest`) and adds two new public items (`RequestId`, `SetRequestIdLayer`).

## Step 3: Serve-to-response lifecycle (7 layers)

1. **`axum::serve(listener, make_service)`** — `axum/src/serve/mod.rs:103`. Returns `Serve<L, M, S, B, TokioExecutor>`. `Serve::run` (line 321) loops calling `listener.accept().await` (line 333) then `handle_connection(...)` (334). Graceful variant: `WithGracefulShutdown::run` at line 447.
2. **Listener accept** — `axum/src/serve/listener.rs:39` (`Listener` trait `accept`) plus per-listener impls (TcpListener at 59, UnixListener at 142, etc.). Yields `(io, remote_addr)`.
3. **`make_service`** — In `handle_connection` (`axum/src/serve/mod.rs:559`): `make_service.ready().await` (582), then `make_service.call(IncomingStream { io: &io, remote_addr }).await` (587). For `Router::into_make_service()`, this `Service<IncomingStream>` impl is at `axum/src/routing/mod.rs:579` (`impl<L> Service<serve::IncomingStream<'_, L>> for Router<()>`), whose `call` (591) returns `ready(Ok(self.clone().with_state(())))`. The result is a per-connection `Router<()>` service. Hyper drives it via `TowerToHyperService` (596) inside `builder.serve_connection_with_upgrades(io, hyper_service)` (613).
4. **`Router::call` → `Router::call_with_state`** — `axum/src/routing/mod.rs:599` defines `Service<Request<B>> for Router<()>`; `call` at 614 wraps body and delegates to `self.call_with_state(req, ())`. `call_with_state` (line 452) calls `self.inner.path_router.call_with_state(...)` and falls through to `catch_all_fallback.call_with_state` (458) on `NotFound`.
5. **`PathRouter::call_with_state`** — `axum/src/routing/path_router.rs:325`. Sets `OriginalUri` in extensions (334–337), calls `self.node.at(parts.uri.path())` (matchit, 342), inserts `MatchedPath` via `set_matched_path_for_request` (347) and url params via `insert_url_params` (353), looks up the endpoint, and either calls `method_router.call_with_state(req, state)` (363) or `route.clone().call_owned(req)` (365).
6. **`MethodRouter::call_with_state`** — `axum/src/routing/method_routing.rs:1167`. Matches `req.method()` (1174) and dispatches to one of the per-method `MethodEndpoint`s: `Route(route)` → `route.clone().oneshot_inner_owned($req)` (1178); `BoxedHandler(handler)` → `handler.clone().into_route(state)` then `oneshot_inner_owned` (1181–1182). Falls back to `fallback.call_with_state` (1215). For routing called as a handler, see also `impl<S> Handler<(), S> for MethodRouter<S>` at line 1355.
7. **`Handler::call` and `IntoResponse`** — At the bottom of the stack, a `BoxedHandler`'s `into_route(state)` produces a `Route` wrapping a `HandlerService` whose `Service::call` (`axum/src/handler/service.rs:165`) invokes `Handler::call(handler, req, state)`. Inside the `impl_handler!`-generated `Handler::call` (`axum/src/handler/mod.rs:238`), extractors run, the user closure is invoked (line 255), and `IntoResponse::into_response()` converts the return value. `Route` itself is a `BoxCloneSyncService<Request, Response, E>` (`axum/src/routing/route.rs:34`) wrapped in `MapIntoResponse` (44) so anything `IntoResponse` becomes a `Response`. The future bubbles back through `RouteFuture` (`axum/src/routing/future.rs`) to hyper, which serializes the `Response`.

## Step 4: Adding a request context / ID layer

**Recommendation: Extension-layer approach** (least breaking).

Why: axum already has the precedent — `MatchedPath` and `OriginalUri` are written into `parts.extensions` *inside* `PathRouter::call_with_state` (`axum/src/routing/path_router.rs:334–337` for `OriginalUri`; `347` for `MatchedPath` via `set_matched_path_for_request`). Extractors then read from `parts.extensions`:

- `MatchedPath` extractor: `axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:73–78` reads `parts.extensions.get::<Self>()`.
- `Extension<T>` extractor: `axum/src/extension.rs:83–98` reads `parts.extensions.get::<T>()`.

So the existing pattern for "request-scoped data threaded everywhere" *is* request extensions. A request-ID layer should follow the same playbook.

**Edit map (least-breaking — new module, no touch to Handler/Router internals):**

1. **New extractor + ID type** — new file `axum/src/extract/request_id.rs`:
   - `pub struct RequestId(Arc<str>)` with `as_str()` accessor (mirroring `MatchedPath` at `axum/src/extract/matched_path.rs:57`).
   - `impl<S: Send + Sync> FromRequestParts<S> for RequestId` reading `parts.extensions.get::<Self>()` (mirror `axum/src/extension.rs:83`); rejection variant `RequestIdMissing` defined via `define_rejection!` (`axum-core/src/macros.rs`).
   - `impl<S> OptionalFromRequestParts<S> for RequestId` returning `Ok(extensions.get::<Self>().cloned())` (mirror matched_path.rs:84).
2. **Wire the extractor module** — `axum/src/extract/mod.rs:14` (add `mod request_id;`), `axum/src/extract/mod.rs:30` (re-export `pub use self::request_id::RequestId;`).
3. **Rejection types** — `axum/src/extract/rejection.rs` gains a `RequestIdMissing` rejection following the file's existing `composite_rejection!` / `define_rejection!` patterns.
4. **`SetRequestIdLayer` middleware** — new file `axum/src/middleware/request_id.rs` implementing `tower_layer::Layer<S>` whose service reads/generates an ID (from a header like `x-request-id`, falling back to a uuid) and inserts it into `req.extensions_mut()` before delegating to the inner service. Pattern matches what `axum/src/middleware/from_fn.rs` and `axum/src/middleware/map_request.rs` already do.
5. **Optional: integrate into `Router`** by exposing `Router::with_request_id_layer()` as sugar, applied via existing `Router::layer` (`axum/src/routing/mod.rs:296`). Not strictly necessary — `router.layer(SetRequestIdLayer::new())` already works because `Router::layer`'s bound is `Layer<Route>`.

**Tradeoffs (axum-specific surfaces):**

- **`Router::layer` vs `Router::route_layer`** (`axum/src/routing/mod.rs:296` vs `313`): `layer` applies to *every* request including 404/405 fallbacks (the layer is applied to both `path_router` and `catch_all_fallback`, lines 305–307). `route_layer` (321) only wraps matched routes — it skips the `catch_all_fallback` branch. For a request-ID layer you want IDs on 404s too, so **use `Router::layer`** (or a top-level `serve`-level wrap), matching the `MatchedPath`/`OriginalUri` pattern of running *before* path matching.
- **Layer ordering vs path-router internals**: `MatchedPath` is set inside `PathRouter::call_with_state` (`axum/src/routing/path_router.rs:347`), so any user `Router::layer` runs *before* `MatchedPath` is in extensions. A request-ID layer added via `Router::layer` is fine because it only needs to *insert* an extension and doesn't need `MatchedPath`. If you want the request-ID to be available in span fields *along with* matched path (e.g. for tracing), you compose: `Router::layer(TraceLayer::new_for_http().make_span_with(|req| { ... }))` where the span function pulls both.
- **Handler trait modification (rejected):** would require changing the trait signature at `axum/src/handler/mod.rs:148` plus every impl listed in Step 1 (six core impls plus the macro expansion for 16 arities, plus `axum-extra` impls). Cascades into `HandlerService` at `axum/src/handler/service.rs:165`. Hard breaking change for zero user benefit since extensions already thread through.
- **New extractor type only, no layer (rejected):** would force every handler to also accept a `RequestId` extractor without anything populating it. Without the layer, `FromRequestParts` returns `Err(RequestIdMissing)` and you get a 5xx — same failure mode as forgetting `.layer(Extension(...))`.

The extension-layer approach reuses every existing surface (`Router::layer`, `parts.extensions`, the `FromRequestParts` blanket promotion to `FromRequest`) and adds two new public items (`RequestId`, `SetRequestIdLayer`).
